facts.text: Crayola’s roots trace back to Peekskill, where the Binney & Smith Company—now Crayola LLC—originated from a 1864 chemical firm founded by Joseph Binney.
Hudson Valley Hospital Center began as Peekskill Hospital in 1889 on lower South Street, serving the community for over a century.
In August 1949, a planned benefit concert for the Civil Rights Congress in Peekskill was canceled after anti‑communist violence erupted.
The Paramount Center for the Arts, a restored 1930 movie palace, reopened in 2013 as the Paramount Hudson Valley Theater, keeping Peekskill’s cultural scene alive.
Peekskill High School has produced NBA stars, including Elton Brand, who graduated before becoming a league All‑Star.
During the Revolutionary War, George Washington briefly set up his headquarters in Peekskill after leaving New Windsor in June 1781.
